title: हनुमान पञ्चरत्नम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान पञ्चरत्नम् ॥

वीतरागं जितक्रोधं सदा ब्रह्मपरायणम्।
श्रीरामभक्तं धीरं तं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥१॥

नानारत्नसमायुक्तं कुण्डलद्वयभूषितम्।
सर्वदा रामसेवार्थं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥२॥

उद्यद्दिवाकरद्युतिं महाबलपराक्रमम्।
दैत्यनाशकरं वीरं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

सागरोल्लङ्घनं कृत्वा लङ्कामारोप्य लीलया।
सीतायाः शोकनाशार्थं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

लक्ष्मणप्राणदातारं संजीवनसमानयम्।
रामेष्टं कपिश्रेष्ठं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
पञ्चरत्नमिदं पुण्यं हनूमत्प्रीतिकारकम्।
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥
आयुरारोग्यमैश्वर्यं बलं तेजो विवर्धनम्।
हनूमत्पञ्चरत्नस्य पाठनात्सर्वसिद्धिदम्॥

॥ इति श्री शंकराचार्यविरचितं श्री हनुमान पञ्चरत्नम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Pancharatnam ||

Vita-ragam Jita-krodham Sada Brahma-parayanam.
Shri Rama-bhaktam Dhiram tam Hanumantam namamyaham.||1||

Nana-ratna-samayuktam Kundala-dwaya-bhushitam.
Sarvada Rama-sevartham Hanumantam namamyaham.||2||

Udyad-divakara-dyutim Maha-bala-parakramam.
Daitya-nashakaram Viram Hanumantam namamyaham.||3||

Sagarollan-ghanam kritva Lankam-aropya lilaya.
Sitayah shoka-nashartham Hanumantam namamyaham.||4||

Lakshmana-prana-dataram Sanjivana-samanayam.
Rameshtam Kapi-shreshtham Hanumantam namamyaham.||5||

|| Phalashruti ||
Pancharatnam-idam punyam Hanumat-priti-karakam.
Yah pathet pratar-utthaya sarva-papaih pramuchyate.
Ayur-arogya-maishwaryam balam tejo vivardhanam.
Hanumat-pancharatnasya pathanat sarva-siddhidam.

|| Iti Shri Shankaracharya-virachitam Shri Hanuman Pancharatnam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: वीतराग, जितक्रोध, सदा ब्रह्मपरायण, श्रीरामभक्त, धीर हनुमानजी को नमस्कार। विविध रत्नों से सुशोभित, दो कुण्डलों से भूषित, सदा राम सेवा में लीन हनुमानजी को नमस्कार। उदयकालीन सूर्य जैसी कान्ति वाले, महाबलपराक्रमी, दैत्यनाशक वीर हनुमानजी को नमस्कार। समुद्र लाँघकर लंका पहुँचकर सीताजी के शोक को दूर करने वाले हनुमानजी को नमस्कार। लक्ष्मण के प्राणदाता, संजीवनी लाने वाले, रामप्रिय, कपिश्रेष्ठ हनुमानजी को नमस्कार।
benefits: Five gems of prayer composed by Adi Shankaracharya
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins when recited at dawn
Invokes long life, good health, wealth and strength
Increases inner radiance and spiritual power
Invokes the blessing of all siddhis and pleases hanuman
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Hanuman, Hanuman Pancharatnam (हनुमान पञ्चरत्नम्) stands as five gems of devotional verses. This revered text originates from Adi Shankaracharya. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all sins when recited at dawn and increase inner radiance and spiritual power. It is also said to grant long life, good health, wealth and strength. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti for enhanced blessings. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Pancharatnam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Pancharatnam by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five gems praising Hanuman for health, strength and protection.
relatedSearches: Hanuman Pancharatnam lyrics Sanskrit Devanagari PDF
Vita ragam jita krodham meaning Hanuman
Hanuman Bahuk Tulsidas Sanskrit Hanuman stotra
Hanuman five qualities virat ragam jita krodham

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hanuman Pancharatnam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Pancharatnam is a five-verse Sanskrit hymn (pancha = five, ratnam = jewels — hence 'five jewels') composed by Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE), praising Lord Hanuman through five concentrated meditations. The opening verse Vita-ragam Jita-krodham Sada Brahma-parayanam describes Hanuman as free from passion, conqueror of anger, ever-devoted to Brahman, the supreme Rama-bhakta and a dhira (steadfast) sage. Each subsequent verse meditates on a different aspect: his ornate adornments in service to Rama (verse 2), his rising-sun radiance and demon-slaying valour (verse 3), his ocean-leaping feat and consoling Sita at Ashoka Vatika (verse 4), and his bringing the Sanjeevani mountain to revive Lakshmana (verse 5). Each verse closes with Hanumantam namamy-aham. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya complete works; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Adi Shankara.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the five qualities of Hanuman praised in the Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse encapsulates the five core qualities (pancha-guna) of Hanuman that make him the supreme model for sadhakas: (1) Vita-ragam — completely free from raga (worldly passion and attachment); (2) Jita-krodham — having conquered krodha (anger), the supreme inner enemy; (3) Sada Brahma-parayanam — ever-absorbed in Brahman, the supreme reality; (4) Sri Rama-bhaktam — single-mindedly devoted to Sri Rama; (5) Dhiram — perfectly steadfast, unshakeable in purpose. These five qualities together describe the ideal jnani-bhakta — one who combines absolute Vedantic non-attachment with absolute personal devotion. Adi Shankara's choice of Hanuman as the embodiment of jnana-bhakti unification is itself a profound theological statement. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya; Hindupedia Hanuman virtues; Sundarakanda commentary.)

seoFaqs.question: What episodes of the Ramayana does the Pancharatnam reference?
seoFaqs.answer: The five verses progressively reference Hanuman's most celebrated Ramayana feats: (1) Verse 1 — his constant disposition of vita-raga and jita-krodha that pervades his service to Rama; (2) Verse 2 — his being adorned with various jewels (kundala-dvaya-bhushita) by Sita-Rama in their service; (3) Verse 3 — his udyad-divakara-dyutim (rising-sun radiance) and slaying of various demons including Akshakumara, Lankini, Kalanemi; (4) Verse 4 — his ocean-leap (sagara-ullanghana) to reach Lanka and the destruction of Sita's grief (Sita-shoka-nasha) at Ashoka Vatika; (5) Verse 5 — his lifting and bringing the entire Dronagiri/Sanjeevani mountain (Sanjeevana-samanaya) to revive the dying Lakshmana on the battlefield, the most iconic Hanuman feat. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda and Yuddha Kanda; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Hanuman Pancharatnam called 'five jewels'?
seoFaqs.answer: The title 'Pancharatnam' (five jewels) follows Adi Shankara's signature compositional pattern — he composed several pancharatnam stotras for different deities: Ganesha Pancharatnam, Shiva Pancharatnam, Lakshmi Nrisimha Karavalamba Stotram, Mookapancha-shati and others, each in five concentrated verses. 'Pancha-ratnam' suggests that each of the five verses is a completed, polished, self-contained jewel of meditation — small but supremely valuable. Tradition reads each verse as one ratna in a five-jewel necklace meditatively offered to the deity. The five-verse format is also doctrinally significant — five represents the panchabhuta (five elements), pancha-prana (five vital airs), pancha-kosha (five sheaths) of the human person, suggesting the stotra purifies the entire pancha-fold being. (Sources: Adi Shankara stotra-corpus; Sringeri Sharada Peetham; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Hanuman Pancharatnam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) are the prescribed weekdays. The supreme occasion is Hanuman Jayanti — celebrated twice annually: Chaitra Shukla Purnima (April, the pan-Indian observance) and Margashirsha Krishna Chaturdashi (December-January, the south Indian Tamil-Brahmin observance). Daily recitation should ideally happen at brahma-muhurta (pre-dawn) facing east, with red flowers, sindoor, and a oil-lamp. The phalashruti specifically prescribes pratar-utthaya (reciting upon rising) for sarva-papa-vimukti. Devotees observing Hanuman vrata (Tuesday or 41-day Mandala) chant it 1, 11, 21 or 108 times daily. It is also recited before any tapasya, exam, court case, or critical undertaking to invoke Hanuman's vita-raga jita-krodha disposition. (Sources: Drikpanchang Hanuman Jayanti; Adi Shankara stotras; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Hanuman Pancharatnam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti promises that whoever rises at dawn (pratar-utthaya) and recites this Pancharatnam is freed from all sins (sarva-papaih pramuchyate). Specific benefits invoked include ayuh (long life), arogya (good health), aishwarya (prosperity and wealth), bala (strength — physical and spiritual), tejo-vivardhanam (increase of inner radiance and tejas), Hanumat-priti (the personal pleasure of Hanuman), and sarva-siddhi (all spiritual accomplishments). The recitation is said to grant the practitioner the same five qualities that Hanuman embodies — vita-raga, jita-krodha, brahma-parayanata, rama-bhakti and dhriti — making it a meditative-transformative stotra rather than only a praise. Adi Shankara's Pancharatnam stotras are considered to carry the Acharya's own siddhi-shakti. (Sources: Adi Shankara phalashruti; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Hanuman Pancharatnam differ from Hanuman Chalisa and Sundarakanda?
seoFaqs.answer: These three Hanuman texts differ greatly in language, length and purpose. The Hanuman Pancharatnam (5 Sanskrit verses by Adi Shankara) is a concentrated jnani-bhakti meditation on Hanuman's five core qualities — used by Vedantic and Sri Vidya sadhakas at brahma-muhurta. The Hanuman Chalisa (40 Awadhi-Hindi chaupais by Goswami Tulsidas) is the most-recited Hanuman text in north India, an accessible bhakti-vehicle for daily devotion by anyone. The Sundarakanda is the entire fifth book of Valmiki Ramayana (about 2,800 Sanskrit verses) narrating Hanuman's journey to Lanka — read as parayana (often as Sundarakanda Saptaha or a complete Tuesday-to-Tuesday vrata) for sankata-nivarana (crisis removal). The Pancharatnam suits short concentrated daily japa, the Chalisa suits universal bhakti, and the Sundarakanda suits deep parayana sadhana. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana; Tulsidas works; Adi Shankara stotras.)
